title: rubus ulmifolius var. dalmatinus
intro: Rubus ulmifolius var. dalmatinus, also known as the Dalmatian raspberry or Dalmatian bramble, is a variety of the black raspberry plant native to the Mediterranean region. It is a deciduous shrub with edible fruits and medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various ailments. The plant contains salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, offering health benefits such as pain relief and reduced fever. Rubus ulmifolius var. dalmatinus is often prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and other conditions.
